The Syoma Experience

Discover. Create. Reflect. Connect.

We create creative experiences that bring people together through making, exploring, and sharing. Every workshop is intentionally designed to encourage curiosity, creativity, reflection, and meaningful connection.

Create

Creative Experiences

Using a range of creative mediums, we invite participants to explore ideas, emotions, and perspectives through hands-on experiences.

Art & Illustration

Creative workshops that encourage exploration, expression, and artistic discovery regardless of experience level.

Craft & Handmade

Hands-on experiences that transform simple materials into meaningful personal creations.

Creative Social

Shared experiences that encourage conversation, collaboration, and connection through creativity.

The People Behind Syoma

Two women sharing a belief in the power of creativity to bring people closer to themselves, to one another, and to their communities.

Tumha Dawod

Clinical Psychology Graduate & MHPSS Manager

Tumha has more than seven years of experience designing and managing mental health and psychosocial support programs across Iraq, creating spaces that encourage personal growth, resilience, connection, and community participation.

Ann Antwan

Dentist & Community Development Specialist

Ann has spent the past five years working with humanitarian organizations across Iraq, focusing on youth development, community engagement, leadership, and platforms for young people to express ideas and take meaningful action.

The Symbol

The story behind the Syoma symbol

The Syoma symbol is made of two connected S letters, representing Syoma Studio. The shape creates balance and harmony, with a subtle yin-yang feeling.

The brush within the symbol represents creativity and self-expression — a reminder that creativity is not only for artists. It is something everyone can use to explore, reflect, and connect.
